result.summary: ThoughtSpot exposes tenant-hosted REST APIs (v2.0 and deprecated v1) plus the hosted ThoughtSpot Spotter MCP server at `https://agent.thoughtspot.app/mcp` and its bearer-token variant at `https://agent.thoughtspot.app/token/mcp`; all require authentication.
result.description: ThoughtSpot is an analytics platform for searching, analyzing, and embedding governed business data and AI-driven insights. Its cloud and software deployments expose APIs for administration, metadata, search, reporting, and AI analytics workflows.

ts_mcp_oauth_client.label: ThoughtSpot Spotter MCP OAuth client
ts_mcp_oauth_client.setup: For remote MCP access, register an OAuth client from the **ThoughtSpot MCP OAuth Client Registration** flow referenced in [Connecting MCP Server to clients](https://developers.thoughtspot.com/docs/connect-mcp-server-to-clients). When registering the client, add your ThoughtSpot instance URL to the allowlist / configuration as instructed there, then use the resulting OAuth client with the Spotter MCP server at `https://agent.thoughtspot.app/mcp`.

ts_auth_token.label: ThoughtSpot full access token (TS_AUTH_TOKEN)
ts_auth_token.setup: In your ThoughtSpot instance, enable trusted authentication and call the REST API endpoint `/api/rest/2.0/auth/token/full` as described in [REST API v2.0 authentication](https://developers.thoughtspot.com/docs/api-authv2). The returned full access token (`TS_AUTH_TOKEN`) is then sent as a bearer token to REST API v2.0 calls, or used for bearer-token MCP access as shown in [Integrating Spotter MCP Server in a custom application or chatbot](https://developers.thoughtspot.com/docs/custom-chatbot-integration-mcp).

ts_session_cookie.label: ThoughtSpot session cookie login
ts_session_cookie.setup: Log in to your ThoughtSpot cluster using the cookie-based login flow documented in [REST API v2.0 authentication](https://developers.thoughtspot.com/docs/api-authv2), or the deprecated v1 login flow at [REST API v1 authentication](https://developers.thoughtspot.com/docs/api-auth-session). A successful login returns ThoughtSpot session cookies, which must be included in subsequent API requests.

ts_basic_login.label: ThoughtSpot username and password
ts_basic_login.setup: Use a ThoughtSpot user account's `username` and `password` with the deprecated v1 login endpoint `/tspublic/v1/session/login` as described in [REST API v1 authentication](https://developers.thoughtspot.com/docs/api-auth-session). This login returns session cookies used on later v1 API requests.
